name: plan-guard
description: Sidecar plugin that enforces step-by-step plan locked-decision adherence — the marker + hook + linter triad that closes the three observed plan-drift root causes (orchestrator escape in sub-agent prompts · mid-design drift off the original plan · sbs Auto-QA conformance being a soft self-check that misses it). CORE (0.1.0): the `plan-lint` engine (`_plan_lint.hexa`, via `hexa run`) parses a plan.md's `## locked decisions` `@L<n> … assert:<kind> <arg>` machine-readable contract (step-by-step 0.8.0 marker spec) and verifies each assert against the repo or, with `--diff <files…>`, a restricted file set: `grep <pat>` (term present · `!pat` = absent) · `file <path>` (exists) · `verdict <slug>/<id>` (`.verdicts/<slug>/<id>.txt` non-empty). Prints a per-`@L` ✓/✗ verdict and exits NONZERO when any assert is unmet; a plan with no `@L` lines is skipped (exit 0, backward-compatible). Advisory by design (the linter exits nonzero so a caller can surface it, but the bundled hooks only ever emit additionalContext — never deny). Mirrors the memory-lint hexa-lint pattern (defensive readers, JSON-escaped additionalContext, no network). Hooks (PreToolUse(Agent) verbatim injection + Stop/PostToolUse ship-time lint) ship in follow-up versions.
